name: Set a Travel Notice or Unlock a Card Flagged for Fraud
description: Call the card issuer to either (a) place a proactive travel notice before a trip or (b) clear a fraud-flag block while the user is already mid-purchase or mid-trip, restoring card function quickly without weakening fraud protection.
disclaimer: Not financial or tax advice. Agent must NEVER share SSN unprompted, NEVER initiate wires above the operator's stated cap, NEVER agree to a hardship plan / settlement that affects credit without explicit ask_operator approval.

context.when_to_use: Use BEFORE a trip to add a travel notice (still helpful with many issuers, even though some — Chase, AmEx, Cap One — no longer require it). Use IN THE MOMENT when a card has been declined or locked, the user is at a register, hotel desk, or trying to book travel, and needs the block lifted.
preconditions: User has the card in hand (or knows the card last 4).
User can describe the failed transaction: amount, merchant, approximate time, location.
User is calling the number on the BACK of the card — not a number from a text/email alert.
If pre-trip: dates, countries / states, and approximate spend levels are ready.

principles: If the trigger was an 'unusual activity alert' text — DO NOT click the link. Call the card-back number directly. Scammers blast fake alerts to spoof the bank.
Confirm 'is this an actual hold, or is the merchant declining for their own reason?' Banks blame merchants and vice versa; the user wastes time if you call the wrong party.
Pre-trip notices reduce false positives but aren't bulletproof. Have a backup payment method.
When a transaction is blocked, the rep will usually ask you to confirm the SPECIFIC declined attempt — be ready with the amount and merchant.
Do NOT ask the rep to disable fraud monitoring entirely. The right ask is 'whitelist this trip / merchant / amount range', not 'turn the protection off'.
If the user is overseas and locked out, also ask about no-fee international ATM access on this card.

phrases.pre_trip_opener: Hi, I'd like to set a travel notice on my card. I'll be in [countries / states] from [date] to [date], with an expected spend range of [amount]. Can you add the notice and confirm whether your bank still uses them or relies on real-time fraud algorithms?
phrases.in_the_moment_opener: Hi, my card was just declined at [merchant] for [amount] around [time]. I'm trying to confirm whether this is a fraud hold on your end or a merchant decline. Can you check?
phrases.verify_the_attempt: Yes — that decline was me. The transaction is legitimate. Can you authorize the retry and lift the hold?
phrases.confirm_no_blanket_off: Please leave the fraud monitoring on — I just need this transaction / merchant / trip whitelisted, not the protection disabled.
phrases.ask_for_alerts: Can you confirm my alert phone number and email on file, and switch to push-notification approval if available? That way next time I can approve a flagged charge in-app without a phone call.
phrases.international_extras: While we're on the line — for the trip, can you confirm the foreign-transaction fee on this card, daily ATM limits, and whether the bank has partner ATMs in [country] that waive fees?
phrases.graceful_close_unlock: Thanks. Can you confirm the hold is lifted, the previous transaction will go through on retry, the travel notice is in place, and give me your name plus a reference number?
phrases.graceful_close_pretrip: Thanks. Can you confirm the dates and countries on file, that no further action is needed, and that you'd contact me by [phone / push notification] if a charge looks unusual?

tactics.name: Triage: bank hold vs merchant decline
tactics.script: Use `in_the_moment_opener`. Most reps can see in 30 seconds whether the issuer blocked the auth or the merchant's processor returned a decline code. If merchant — call the merchant, not the bank.

tactics.name: Verify the attempt was real
tactics.when: Rep confirms there's a fraud hold.
tactics.script: Use `verify_the_attempt`. Confirming the specific declined attempt is the unlock — once they see the legitimate user verify, the algorithm whitelists similar patterns.

tactics.name: Whitelist, don't disable
tactics.when: Rep offers to 'turn off fraud alerts on this card.'
tactics.script: Use `confirm_no_blanket_off`. Disabling protection broadly creates a much bigger downside if the card is later compromised.

tactics.name: Pre-trip extras
tactics.script: Use `pre_trip_opener` + `international_extras`. Confirm fee structure and ATM partners — saves real money on a multi-week trip.

tactics.name: Upgrade alerts to push
tactics.when: Discussion turns to how the user will be reached next time.
tactics.script: Use `ask_for_alerts`. Push-approval in the bank's app is the fastest unlock channel — usually 1 tap to approve.

tactics.name: Confirm the writeback
tactics.script: Use the matching `graceful_close_*` phrase. Get hold-lifted confirmation, travel-notice dates, and a reference number.

boundaries: Do NOT call any number from an 'unusual activity' text or email — only the card-back number.
Do NOT verify an account by reading back a one-time code that the bank just texted — legitimate reps will never ask for that code.
Do NOT request blanket fraud-monitoring disable — request transaction / merchant / trip-range whitelist only.
Do NOT share full SSN — last 4 of card and last 4 of SSN are typically enough.
Do NOT close the call without a reference number and a confirmed unlock.
success_signals: Rep identifies the specific declined transaction.
Hold lifted, retry authorized to go through.
Travel notice on file with dates and countries.
failure_signals: Rep insists the decline was on the merchant side but the merchant's processor disagrees (you're between two systems — escalate).
Hold cannot be lifted because the account is under suspended status (escalate to fraud team or account-services supervisor).
Travel notice 'isn't supported' AND the algorithm is aggressive (use a backup card abroad).
Caller-ID does not match the card-back number (you may be on a spoofed line — terminate, redial).

exit_strategy.on_success: Confirm hold lifted, travel notice on file, alerts channel, reference number, rep name. Tell the operator the new alert settings.
exit_strategy.on_failure: If unlock fails mid-purchase, the user pays another way and you call back from a quiet location. If the account is suspended, escalate to a fraud / account-services supervisor and request a written summary of what triggered the suspension.
follow_ups: Day +1: confirm the retried transaction posted successfully.
Pre-trip: bring a backup card from a different issuer for redundancy.
Post-trip: remove the notice if the issuer doesn't auto-expire it.

required_user_info: Card last 4 and issuing bank name
Specific declined transaction: amount, merchant, time, location (if mid-purchase)
Travel dates, countries / states, expected spend range (if pre-trip)
Backup payment method in case unlock fails
Operator preference on push-notification vs phone alerts
